Profit/COGS model is genuinely jewelry-specific and authoritative: metal COGS via gold rate-at-time-of-sale, gemstone COGS from child tables, labor/commission/payment/overhead allocation - all persisted once and queried consistently. This is a real differentiator vs generic POS.

performance.py is feature-complete for Workforce Intelligence (scoreboard, targets, compensation engine, quarterly reviews with AI insights) - the backend is ready and is the highest-leverage thing to expose.
Backend permission enforcement is uniform and defense-in-depth (only_for on every endpoint), independent of any frontend gate.
Design tokens (.premium-card, .premium-title, styles/tokens.css) give a consistent visual base to build a unified shell on top of.
data_model: Sale Cost Breakdown (custom doc/child): single authoritative COGS+margin record per Sales Invoice, written by profit_intelligence.calculate_sale_cost_breakdown (hook on invoice submit). Fields: total_metal_cogs, total_gemstone_cogs, total_item_cogs, labor, commission, payment_cost, overhead, gross_profit, gross_margin_pct. All profit queries SUM/AVG over this - no per-query recomputation.
Repair Order: source of truth for repair feed/anomaly (status, promised_date, assigned_to, deposit_amount, estimated_cost, warehouse, modified).
POS Session (pos_session.py): source for active-registers KPI and force-close; emits pos_session_event on open/close.
Performance Log (performance.py:55 _create_performance_log): per-event ledger (sale/layaway/repair/attendance) feeding scoreboard + reviews.

Realtime event channels (logical, not persisted): pos_sale_event (user-scoped to manager), pos_session_event, repair_live_event (global), repair_anomaly_alert (global), employee_event (global, client-filtered), zevar_alert.

profit_intelligence.summary: The backend has a genuinely best-in-class *design* — a single authoritative per-invoice COGS+margin record (Sale Cost Breakdown) capturing metal, gemstone, labor, commission, payment, and overhead costs, with a full JSON calculation log, plus a Pricing Recommendation doctype with RAG auditability fields. BUT the design is critically undermined by an integration failure: the on_submit hook (calculate_sale_cost_breakdown) that populates Sale Cost Breakdown is NEVER registered in hooks.py, so in a live system NO breakdown records are ever created and every query endpoint returns empty/zero. Compounding this: the AI recommendation generator (generate_pricing_recommendations) is also not scheduled; the What-If 'Create Recommendation' button calls a non-existent endpoint; the Approve/Reject buttons pass the wrong action verb; and the Margin Heatmap expects a pivoted data shape the backend never returns. Separately, the COGS model is materially incomplete for jewelry (no making charge, no alloy/wastage, gemstone value is at-cost-not-replacement, labor model is time-guess not actual), margin math is duplicated and inconsistent in 5+ other files (commission payouts are computed on an inflated margin that ignores 4 of the 6 cost buckets), and there is zero margin-waterfall / PVM / multi-store overhead-allocation capability. Net: ~50% of a great system on paper, ~20% functional as shipped.
endpoints: zevar_core.api.profit_intelligence.calculate_sale_cost_breakdown (hook fn, NOT whitelisted, NOT registered in hooks.doc_events — dead code in production)
zevar_core.api.profit_intelligence.create_recommendation (CALLED BY FRONTEND BUT DOES NOT EXIST — 404)
zevar_core.tasks.generate_pricing_recommendations (NOT whitelisted, NOT in scheduler_events — never auto-runs)
zevar_core.tasks.fetch_live_metal_rates (scheduled cron */60, writes Gold Rate Log)
zevar_core.rag.tools.pricing_tools.get_pricing_action_items (whitelisted; AI action feed, not surfaced in Profit UI)
zevar_core.api.pricing._get_gold_rate (internal; canonical gold-rate lookup with purity-alias handling)
current_capabilities: Per-invoice cost-attribution model designed around 6 buckets: metal COGS (net_weight x gold_rate x qty), gemstone COGS (sum of Item Gemstone.amount), labor (from Labor Cost Pool via salesperson splits), commission (from Sales Commission Split), payment processing fees (per-mode % + flat from Cost Center Allocation), overhead (rent+utilities+insurance+marketing+depreciation+miscellaneous) — profit_intelligence.py:71-73.

Gold rate sourced from Gold Rate Log (live, fetched hourly from gold-api.com/metals.live/goldprice.org/custom-endpoint/fallback chain), with purity-alias normalization (14K vs 14Kt) and Rose/White Gold -> Yellow Gold mapping — pricing.py:333-365.

Materialized results: Sale Cost Breakdown doctype stores total_revenue, all 6 cost components, gold_rate_at_sale + source + timestamp, gross_profit, gross_margin_pct, and a full JSON calculation_log + calculated_by/at audit trail — sale_cost_breakdown.json.
Aggregation/grouping by jewelry_type, metal_type, purity, salesperson, or individual invoice — get_margin_analysis (profit_intelligence.py:539-628).
Period-over-period comparison (current vs previous period revenue/profit/margin) — get_profit_summary:499-513.
Two overhead allocation methods: 'Equal Per Invoice' and 'Proportional to Revenue' — _allocate_overhead:385-457.

Payment-cost allocation by mode: credit (% + flat), debit (%), cash (%), wire (flat), gift card (%), financing partner (%) — _calculate_payment_costs:309-382; configurable in Cost Center Allocation singleton.

Labor allocation via Labor Cost Pool: employee-specific pool, role-based fallback (Sales Associate/Manager/Bench Jeweler/Appraiser), Per-Hour/Per-Sale/Per-Repair/Salary-Spread methods — labor_cost_pool.json; allocated by salesperson split_percent x default_minutes_per_sale.

What-if price simulator: current vs projected margin, COGS split (metal+gemstone), price-change %, annualized profit projection from 90-day sales velocity, optional gold-rate override — pricing_tools.simulate_price_change:147-254.

Pricing Recommendation doctype with full RAG auditability fields: rag_query_id, similar_events_count, rag_sources, confidence_level, reasoning, gold_rate_at_generation, valid_until, and an approve->auto-apply workflow that writes custom_msrp — pricing_recommendation.json; review_recommendation:845-878.

Rule-based recommendation generator (declining-margin detection + slow-moving clearance) writing Pricing Recommendation records with AI-style reasoning — tasks.generate_pricing_recommendations:889-1059.

Vue 3 dashboard with 4 tabs (Overview/Cost Analysis/Pricing/Margin Heatmap), Pinia store, KPI cards with trend arrows, stacked cost-component bar chart (absolute/% toggle), monthly profit trend bars, recommendation table with approve/reject, what-if simulator panel.
Role-based access control via frappe.only_for on every query endpoint (System Manager / Store Manager / Sales Manager / Accounts Manager).
strengths: Architectural purity of the data model: ONE authoritative per-invoice record (Sale Cost Breakdown) that every query reads via SUM/AVG — no per-query recomputation on the read path. This is the correct pattern and makes the numbers auditable and reconcilable.

Full calculation traceability: calculation_log stores a step-by-step JSON of revenue/cogs/labor/commission/payment/overhead, plus gold_rate_source (live/fallback/manual) and gold_rate_timestamp — defensible for owner/CPA questions and for audit.
Gold-rate-at-sale is point-in-time captured (not re-fetched at query time), so historical margins stay accurate even as spot moves — a genuinely correct jewelry-specific decision.
Purity-alias normalization (14K<->14Kt) and Rose/White->Yellow mapping in _get_gold_rate prevents silent zero-COGS rows that plague naive implementations.

Pricing Recommendation doctype's RAG-auditability fields (rag_query_id, similar_events_count, rag_sources) are best-in-class design — they let an owner see WHY the AI suggested a price and what historical events grounded it. This is the single strongest differentiator vs. generic retail tools.
Payment-cost allocation is mode-aware and includes financing-partner cut (Affirm/Klarna) — rare even in dedicated jewelry systems.

The what-if simulator correctly uses net_weight x gold_rate (not valuation_rate) for metal COGS, and surfaces annualized profit projection from real velocity — the right basis for a pricing conversation.
Cost Center Allocation is a singleton with sensible defaults (2.9% CC, 0.30 flat, etc.) and labor_burden_percent + operating_hours_daily fields ready for driver-based overhead.
Role-gated endpoints and a clean Pinia store with parallel resource fetching (Promise.all in loadAll) — the frontend plumbing is well-organized for its scope.
gaps: CRITICAL — DATA FRESHNESS: calculate_sale_cost_breakdown is NOT registered in hooks.py doc_events for Sales Invoice on_submit/on_cancel. grep across the whole repo finds it only at its definition (profit_intelligence.py:21) and the manual recalculate_breakdown caller (:734). The Sales Invoice on_submit list in hooks.py registers commission, stock_reduction, and reservation_manager but NOT profit_intelligence. Therefore in a deployed system, Sale Cost Breakdown records are NEVER auto-created, and get_profit_summary/get_margin_analysis/get_margin_heatmap/get_cost_component_trends ALL return empty/zero. The entire dashboard is blank unless an admin manually runs recalculate_breakdown per invoice. This is the #1 issue.

CRITICAL — NO on_cancel cleanup: there is no hook to delete/void Sale Cost Breakdown when a Sales Invoice is cancelled. Even if on_submit were wired, cancelled invoices would leave orphan breakdowns inflating profit. (The on_submit body self-deletes prior breakdowns for amend scenarios at :31, but cancel has no handler anywhere.)

CRITICAL — Recommendation engine never runs: generate_pricing_recommendations (tasks.py:889) is defined but NOT in scheduler_events (hooks.py scheduler only lists fetch_live_metal_rates for */60 cron). So Pricing Recommendation records are never auto-generated; the dashboard's Pricing tab is permanently empty unless an admin manually invokes the function.

CRITICAL — Broken 'Create Recommendation' button: WhatIfSimulator.vue:217 calls zevar_core.api.profit_intelligence.create_recommendation, which does not exist anywhere in the codebase (grep for 'def create_recommendation' returns nothing). The button throws a 404/wrong-method error.

CRITICAL — Broken Approve/Reject buttons: PricingRecommendationsPanel.vue:113 calls handleReview(rec.name, 'Approved') and confirmReject passes 'Rejected', but review_recommendation (profit_intelligence.py:846-876) checks `if action == 'approve'` / `elif action == 'reject'` (lowercase) and throws 'Invalid action' otherwise. Casing mismatch -> every click hits the frappe.throw branch.

CRITICAL — Margin Heatmap data-shape mismatch: backend get_margin_heatmap (profit_intelligence.py:739-773) returns a FLAT array of {jewelry_type, metal_type, revenue, gross_profit, avg_margin, count}. Frontend MarginHeatmap.vue (rows/cols/getCellValue at :97-122) expects a PIVOTED structure with row.margins[metalType].margin_pct. The pivot never exists, so columns is always empty and every cell renders '--'. The heatmap tab is non-functional.

BUG — Confidence bar always 0%: PricingRecommendationsPanel.vue treats rec.confidence as a number (lines 99, 103, 219-223 use c>=80), but the doctype field is confidence_level = Select('High'|'Medium'|'Low') and get_recommendations does not return a numeric confidence. Bar renders gray/0% for every row.

COGS model gap — NO making charge: jewelry profitability fundamentally includes making/maintenance charges (per-gram or per-piece), which can be 10-30% of price. Not modeled anywhere in Sale Cost Breakdown (no field, no allocation). This is the largest jewelry-specific accuracy hole.

COGS model gap — NO alloy/wastage: net_weight x gold_rate assumes pure metal at marked purity with no manufacturing loss. Real jewelry COGS applies wastage % (typically 5-12%) and alloy mix. Not modeled.

COGS model gap — gemstone value is static Item-master cost, not replacement/lot cost: _get_gemstone_value (profit_intelligence.py:193-203) sums Item Gemstone.amount — a fixed field. It ignores melee-parcel lot costing, exchange-rate moves on imported stones, and certificate premium. Aged inventory's gemstones are often under-costed vs replacement.

COGS model gap — labor is a guessed constant, not actual: _allocate_labor (profit_intelligence.py:206-296) uses default_minutes_per_sale (default 30) x hourly_rate, scaled by split_percent. It ignores ticket complexity, bench-jeweler repair labor on the same sale, and the labor_burden_percent field defined in Cost Center Allocation is NEVER applied (the field exists but _allocate_labor reads only hourly_rate, not hourly_rate x (1+burden)).

Margin by STORE — not supported: get_margin_analysis groups by jewelry_type/metal_type/purity/salesperson/invoice only. There is no store/branch dimension despite Cost Center Allocation being store-relevant. No multi-store profit consolidation.
Margin by CATEGORY (beyond jewelry_type) — partial: no item_group/brand/collection grouping; jewelry_type is a single custom field.

No Price/Volume/Mix (PVM) bridge: period-over-period profit change is shown as a single delta, not decomposed into price effect vs volume effect vs mix effect vs cost effect. This is table-stakes for a 'Profit Intelligence' exec product.

No margin-waterfall visualization: nowhere does the UI show revenue -> minus metal -> minus gems -> minus labor -> minus commission -> minus payment -> minus overhead -> gross profit as a waterfall. The CostBreakdownChart shows the components as a stacked bar but not the bridge.

No gold-rate pass-through analysis: no view of 'how much of recent margin change is explained by gold moving +/-X%' vs pricing discipline. The pricing_tools.get_pricing_action_items detects gold moves >3% but does not quantify pass-through %.
No unrealized gain/loss on inventory: gold held in stock appreciates/depreciates with spot; no MTM view. (Out of scope for COGS but expected in a 'Profit Intelligence' suite.)

No markdown/velocity (AIMS) engine: Pricing Recommendation supports a 'Clearance' type, but there is no aged-inventory spiff+markdown+signage algorithm — just a flat 15% markdown for >90-day slow movers (tasks.py:1032).
No margin-floor enforcement at POS: nothing prevents a POS sale below a configured minimum margin; the breakdown is computed after the fact only.
No interchange-tier granularity: payment cost uses one credit_card_rate (2.9%) for all cards — no Visa/MC/Amex tiering, no corporate/regulated signature rates.

Overhead allocation only 2 methods, no ABC/driver-based: 'Equal Per Invoice' and 'Proportional to Revenue'. No activity-based (per-transaction, per-sq-ft, per-headcount) drivers despite operating_hours_daily and labor_burden_percent fields existing unused.

Backed-up UI gaps (capabilities the API has but the UI never exposes): get_cost_breakdown_detail (per-invoice drill-down) has no UI; get_profit_trends quarterly mode not exposed; get_margin_analysis group_by=invoice/metal_type/purity not selectable (UI hardcodes jewelry_type); recalculate_breakdown has no button; pricing_tools.get_pricing_action_items not surfaced; pricing_tools.get_slow_moving_inventory not surfaced; sale-cost-breakdown gold_rate_source/timestamp not shown; RAG auditability fields (rag_sources, similar_events_count) not rendered on recommendation cards.
structural_issues: Integration gap is the dominant structural flaw: the module is a well-designed island with no bridge to the invoice lifecycle. on_submit hook unwired + on_cancel absent + generator unscheduled = the module produces no data automatically. The previous internal audit (plan/02_current_state_audit/module_audit_raw.json:332) incorrectly asserts the hook IS wired and that reports.py does not recompute margin — both claims are false per direct grep. Treat that audit as unreliable.

Margin math is defined 7 different ways across the codebase (see math_integrity / data_model below), only one of which (Sale Cost Breakdown) is 'correct'. Because the SCB path is unwired, the system effectively runs on the WRONG margin definitions (commission.py and top_profitability_by_product.py) wherever margin drives money or reporting today.

The 'what-if' margin (pricing_tools.simulate_price_change) uses a DIFFERENT, thinner COGS than the canonical SCB model — so a price simulated at 28% margin will be recorded as ~22% when the sale actually posts (if SCB were wired). This will destroy user trust in the simulator.

Frontend/backend contract drift with no type-checking: 3 of the 6 frontend interactions with the backend are broken (create_recommendation 404, approve/reject casing, heatmap pivot shape, confidence number-vs-string). There is no OpenAPI/schema contract and no integration test catching these.
Cost Center Allocation is a global singleton — it cannot represent per-store overhead or per-store payment rates, which structurally blocks multi-store profit intelligence even before UI work.

No caching/materialization layer beyond the SCB rows themselves: get_margin_heatmap and get_cost_component_trends run raw GROUP BY SQL over SCB on every page load. Fine at low volume; will degrade without an index hint or rollup table at scale.

Permission asymmetry: get_recommendations is readable by Sales Manager, but review_recommendation allows Store Manager to approve and AUTO-APPLY a price change to Item.custom_msrp (profit_intelligence.py:863-864) — a price-change power with no secondary approval or audit log beyond the doc fields. Defensible but worth noting.
data_model: Sale Cost Breakdown (autoname SCB-YY-MM-#####; unique on sales_invoice): total_revenue, total_qty, gold_rate_at_sale (+source+timestamp), total_metal_cogs, total_gemstone_cogs, total_item_cogs, total_labor_cost (+JSON detail), total_commission, total_payment_cost (+JSON detail), overhead_per_invoice (+method), total_cost, gross_profit, gross_margin_pct, calculation_log (JSON), calculated_by/at. Correct and complete for the 6-bucket model. MISSING fields: making_charge, alloy/wastage, store/cost_center, item_group, gemstone_replacement_vs_cost flag.

Pricing Recommendation (autoname PR-YY-MM-#####): recommendation_type (6 options incl. Clearance & Premium Positioning), current_price/margin_pct/gold_rate, recommended_price, projected_margin_pct, price_change_pct, confidence_level (High/Med/Low), reasoning, rag_query_id/similar_events_count/rag_sources, gold_rate_at_generation, generated_by (default 'AI (Qwen)'), generation_method (rag_pipeline|manual), status lifecycle (Draft->Pending Review->Approved->Applied/Rejected), valid_until, reviewed_by/at, applied_at. Cost Center Allocation (singleton): rent/utilities/insurance/marketing/depreciation/miscellaneous_monthly, overhead_allocation_method (Equal|Proportional), operating_hours_daily, labor_burden_percent (UNUSED by code), payment rates (credit_card_rate+flat, debit, cash_handling, wire_flat, gift_card, financing_partner). Singleton = no per-store.

Labor Cost Pool: pool_name, employee (optional), role (Sales Associate/Bench Jeweler/Store Manager/Appraiser/Manager), hourly_rate ('Fully Loaded'), hours_per_week, allocation_method (Per Hour/Per Sale/Per Repair/Salary Spread), default_minutes_per_sale. allocation_method field is read inconsistently — _allocate_labor always does Per-Sale math regardless of this value.

Item custom fields consumed: custom_net_weight_g, custom_metal_type, custom_purity, custom_gross_weight_g, custom_jewelry_type, custom_msrp, custom_cost_price, valuation_rate (ERPNext), gemstones child table (Item Gemstone: amount).
Sales Commission Split: sales_invoice, employee, commission_amount, split_percent — read by both commission.py and profit_intelligence._get_commission_total.
tech_debt: DRY violation — margin defined 7 ways: (1) profit_intelligence.py:74 [canonical, 6-bucket]; (2) commission.py:104-109 [valuation_rate only, drives real payouts]; (3) top_profitability_by_product.py:70-71 [valuation_rate only, joins SCB but ignores its gross_profit column — ironic]; (4) analytics_hub.py:1012 _margin_pct [custom_cost_price basis]; (5) pricing_tools.py:210 [gold-rate + gems only, no labor/comm/payment/overhead]; (6) repair_accounting.py:704 [material-only, labor excluded as 'pure service']; (7) gemstone_value_service.py:125 [markup-on-cost denominator]. Same key names (gross_profit, margin_pct) across all 7.

commission.py calculate_commissions runs on on_submit BEFORE the (unwired) SCB hook and uses a 1-bucket margin — salespeople on 'By Profit Margin' rules earn commission against an inflated margin. Highest-impact tech debt because it moves money.
top_profitability_by_product report joins Sale Cost Breakdown only to filter, then recomputes a cruder gross_profit from sii.valuation_rate — the canonical number is one column away and ignored.
WhatIfSimulator vs SCB inconsistency: simulator's projected_margin_pct will not match the SCB gross_margin_pct later recorded for the same item (simulator omits 4 cost buckets).
labor_burden_percent and operating_hours_daily fields defined in Cost Center Allocation but never read by _allocate_labor or _allocate_overhead — dead config.
Labor Cost Pool.allocation_method (Per Hour/Per Repair/Salary Spread) ignored — _allocate_labor always computes Per-Sale.

Hardcoded fallback gold price $4400/oz in fetch_live_metal_rates (tasks.py:171) vs $3300/oz in pricing._get_hardcoded_fallback_rates (pricing.py:210) — two different fallback numbers for the same concept.

Previous internal audit doc (plan/02_current_state_audit/module_audit_raw.json) records two false claims (hook is wired; no margin duplication) — the planning artifacts are built on an incorrect baseline and should be corrected before redesign work proceeds.
get_margin_heatmap and get_cost_component_trends use DATE_FORMAT string grouping in raw SQL with no rollup/materialization — acceptable now, scaling risk later.
Frontend uses ad-hoc frappe.call (WhatIfSimulator.vue:190) bypassing the frappe-ui resource pattern used by the store — inconsistent data-fetching style.

math_integrity.canonical_definition: profit_intelligence.py:71-75 — gross_profit = total_revenue - (metal_cogs + gemstone_cogs + labor + commission + payment_cost + overhead); margin = gross_profit / total_revenue * 100. Metal_cogs = net_weight * gold_rate * qty (gold_rate from Gold Rate Log, point-in-time). This is the ONLY definition that includes all 6 cost buckets.

Even after wiring the hook, 6 other sites will continue to show different numbers for 'margin' until the shared profit_math refactor is done.

Time & Day Report: retail sales broken down by day-of-week AND time-of-day — the core hourly/trend analytics view, pairs with TraxSales traffic for conversion analysis.
Salesperson Performance Report (see Workforce) doubles as channel/associate sales-performance analytics.
Frequently Sold Items report + Top 'N' Customer Report: best-seller and top-customer analytics.
Vendor Style Analysis Report & 20/80 vendor insight: official claim that stores discover ~20% of customers = 80% of sales, and sales concentrate in a handful of vendors; reports quantify this.
Automated Reports Feature: scheduled/emailed reports (memorized report definitions).
Tender Detail Report & Custom Tender Detail Report: payment-method mix analytics (cash/card/financing/store-account).
Sales Tax Report, Statements and Aging, Account Adjustment Report: fiscal sales monitoring.
Advertising Reports + Occasions Report (birthdays/anniversaries): marketing-effectiveness vs sales attribution.
Drive Retail & SnapRetail partner integrations: digital/print marketing that drives store traffic, attributable back into sales reports.
profit_intelligence: Per-item cost & sale price WITH margins visible in inventory; shows 'the lowest price you can accept' (floor-price / negotiation floor) when a customer haggles.

Gross Profit Performance in Salesperson report: GP $, GP% (profit as % of total sale), and % Markup (markup multiplier on RETAIL price, e.g. cost $48 → sell $100 = 52% GP = 109% markup). This is true jewelry COGS→margin intelligence.

Open to Buy (OTB) wizard: generates an entire purchasing schedule for an upcoming sales period from past data, sales estimates, current on-hand inventory, and on-order inventory; forecast weighted by the previous 3 years of buying periods; separates Total COGS, Special Orders COGS, Repeat Sales COGS, Net COGS, and (COGS) Sales estimate. This is forward profit/capital-allocation intelligence.

Aged Inventory Management System (AIMS): monthly run that applies tiered spiffs (bonus commissions) and markdowns at specific age thresholds to liquidate old stock; produces preview of Old vs New values, generates a Bonus Poster (staff-facing spiff signage) and in-case signage (customer-facing clearance). Direct margin-recovery tool.
Weekly Buying Plan: pairs with AIMS to enforce 'just in time' smaller/smarter reorders — profitability via inventory turn optimization.

Spot Metal Pricing: live spot pricing for gold/silver/platinum/palladium (Kitco-linked); at POS optionally prompts for current metal price (configurable timeout); each store metal mapped to a Metal Type + purity value (e.g. 10k = 41% gold); updating metal price reprices all relevant items instantly. Critical for true gold/metal COGS and dynamic pricing.
Inventory Buying Tools + Pricing Report + Item Pricing Wizard + Reprice Items: systematic margin & repricing toolset.
Vendor analysis reports: vendor-level profitability and sell-through to cut unprofitable lines.
Pre-Appraisals & Appraisals modules: valuation/insurance records tied to cost basis.
Trade In / Buying module with actionable reports for gold/estate buying profitability.
Returns reporting with $ GP lost to returns (profit leak visibility).

Credit-card-processing transparency is minimal on the vendor site (integrated but opaque terms).
Reports are heavy/static (desktop reporting framework) rather than real-time interactive dashboards — the Dashboards module is configurable but not a true streaming live wall.
Single-vertical focus only on independent owner-operated jewelers (1–5 stores, ≤~20 users); not built for chains, multi-region, or e-commerce-first operators — limits scalability story.

ux_patterns: Parametric Report Creator: a single search-driven catalog (Reporting :: Report Creator) where every report shares the same File Options -> Report Options -> Report Criteria -> Printing Options -> Aging Options flow. Predictable, composable, trainable.

Persistent criteria vocabulary across all reports (By Store / State / Supplier / Class / Color / SKU / Col / Row / PO# / Coupon / Custom Entry) with comma-separated multi-select + per-field Exclude checkbox — users learn the filter grammar once.
Sort/Order selector ('In Order Of') baked into every ranking report: by Qty Sold / Net Sales / Profit / ROI / Turns / Inventory Value / Sell-Thru %.
'Analyze By' (dimension) x 'Report Type' (granularity: SKU Detail / Class Summary / Supplier Summary / Price Point Summary) x 'Group By' — three orthogonal axes for slicing the same dataset.
Store-Options toggle (Separate / Combine / Compare / Store Summary) appears on nearly every report — multi-store comparison is a first-class, ubiquitous control, not a separate screen.
Period-over-period via Report Column 1 / Report Column 2 (side-by-side sales-period comparison) and up to 4 sales periods on Stock Status.
Output-first: every report lets you pick File Type (incl. CSV w/ custom delimiter), name it, and Email-A-Copy-To at generation time — built for sharing/export, not just on-screen viewing.
Scheduled email distribution: assign any report to any person on any cadence ('as often or as little as you'd like') — ops-leadership digest pattern.
Opt-in richness via 'Also Print' checkboxes (Costs, ROI & Turns, Age, Sales History by Month, Location, Perks, Column Totals) — keeps the default report clean, power users layer on.
Grid (matrix) analytics: column/row model with % of total and sell-thru per cell — strong pattern for any attribute-matrix retail (transfers to jewelry karat/size/gem grids).
data_model_hints: SKU is the atomic item, extended by a GRID: column (Col) x row (Row) dimensions (built for apparel size matrices — Zevar would repurpose Col/Row for karat x size, or gem-type x setting).
Class hierarchy is multi-level with a 'Class Depth Limit' parameter (report at top class or drill to lowest leaf) — implies a tree structure on categories.
Supplier and Color are first-class item attributes; 'Product Custom Entries' are user-defined extra attributes (a generic extension point Zevar could use for metal/gem/cert).
Per-SKU pricing model carries: Retail Price, Markdown Price, and 'Perk' (a third pricing/promo layer) — three parallel price fields with 'as of today' report filters.
Per-SKU stock model carries: On-Hand, Model (target), Max, Reorder, plus derived Short (On-Hand - Model) and Critical (Short AND not on order) — a min/max/reorder-point replenishment model.
On-Order is split temporally: At Once (<=30 days) vs Future (>30 days) vs Total — the 30-day threshold is a store setting.

Inventory ledger uses an enumerated Inventory Change Type (Transfer Out/In, In-Transit, PO Receipt, Physical Count, Receipts w/out PO, Return to Supplier, Store Sales/COGS, Adjustments, Into/From Non-Sellable) — each transaction is typed; clean audit trail and a strong template for Zevar's stock ledger doctype.
Non-Sellable is a separate inventory state items move Into/From — useful pattern for damaged/dirty gold or pulled-for-repair stock.
Multi-store org has a Store Owner level that unlocks cross-store Report Criteria (implies role-tied data scoping); In-Transit (transferred-not-received) is its own quantity bucket.
User entity has statuses: Unapproved / Active / Disabled / Deleted, all reportable — lifecycle states on the salesperson/staff record.
Salesperson == Cashier (User Code) with optional separate crediting; tickets carry a Sale Type (Store / Special Order / Layaway / Employee / Return) at the Ticket Header.
Two ticket-counting modes (Simple count vs signed-quantity Calculated) implies line items carry a signed quantity and a transaction-type code on each ticket.

Frequent Buyer plan is a config entity: Calculation Type, Break Point (Qty or Amt), Accumulating Classes, min-price & max-markdown guards, Points Factor, Graduation Plan, Expiry + Grace, Redemption-on-current-vs-next — a flexible rules engine for loyalty, stored per-plan and referenced from the customer record.
Frequent Buyer accumulation/reward state lives on the customer record (deleting a plan nulls the customer's plan and wipes their accumulation/rewards).
differentiators: Deep, parametric specialty-retail reporting vocabulary (185+ reports) with a single consistent filter grammar shared across every report — unusually uniform and trainable for multi-store operators.

Best-in-class salesperson add-on/attachment analytics: explicit base-class + up-to-3-add-on-class report, plus a class-exclusion control to keep multi-sale/UPT rates honest — a genuinely smart UPT/attachment coaching tool.
ROI and Turns computed and exposed per SKU/salesperson with documented formulas, plus tri-mode inventory valuation (Active Price / Average Cost / Current Cost) on the same report.
Grid (matrix) analytics with per-cell sell-thru and % of total — purpose-built for attribute-matrix retail (sizes now; directly portable to jewelry karat/gem/size grids).
Ubiquitous multi-store comparison mode (Separate / Combine / Compare / Store Summary) on essentially every report and a Store-Owner role tier that gates cross-store reporting.

Flexible native loyalty rules engine (5 methods, graduation plans, expiry/grace, accumulation guards) without needing a third-party app.
Cloud + true offline-first POS with auto-sync; lightweight mobile POS (RICS>Checkout) for peak-traffic queue-busting.

research_compact.vendor: Lightspeed Retail (Lightspeed Commerce) — X-Series / R-Series / S-Series (formerly ShopKeep) retail POS, with the Lightspeed Insights / Advanced Reporting analytics layer. Broad retail POS with a dedicated jewelry vertical. Sourced primarily from official Lightspeed help docs, the product/analytics marketing pages, and G2/Capterra user reviews.

live_monitoring: Lightspeed Insights Live / Pulse app: real-time sales & revenue tracking on a mobile-first owner/manager dashboard (Pulse is most mature on the hospitality/K-Series line, where it shows live sales, staff performance, multi-location oversight, and trend detection; retail variants exist but the 'Live' real-time tier is gated to the Custom/Insights plan on O-Series).

Retail Dashboard (X-Series, Pro/Advanced/Enterprise + Core/Plus): manager/admin dashboard across all or selected outlets with View-by day/week/month, date range, outlet multi-select, and a rich 'More filters' panel (Product name, SKU, category, tag, variant attribute, brand, supplier, customer, customer group, promotion, register, user). Sales graph with selectable x-axis granularity: Hours, Hours-of-Day (default), Days, Day-of-Week, Weeks, Months, Years — plus a toggle to 'Average Sales by Hour' instead of totals. Good template for a live sales timeline.

Sales Summary email: a scheduled end-of-day (midnight-to-11:59pm) recap auto-emailed to the Business Owner early each morning — lightweight 'overnight monitoring' for owners who don't watch a live wall.

Register Summary tile: per-register open/close times, who opened/closed, opening/closing cash, tender totals, pay-ins/payouts, and over/short — directly maps to Zevar's 'system health / employee activity' live feed.
Top Selling Items (top 5 by $), Sales Tenders breakdown, and New vs Returning customer tally tiles on the same dashboard.
sales_monitoring: Period-over-period comparison is a first-class concept: Sales Summary supports a 'Comparison' secondary date/period, and dashboard tiles render the delta inline — enables trend-vs-target and YoY/mo-over-mo analysis without leaving the report.
Sales by Hour of Day report (Advanced/Plus; not on Core): identifies peak transaction periods to optimize staffing — core to Zevar's 'hourly trends / traffic vs sales' requirement.

Multi-dimensional Sales Summary pivots: by Product, SKU name, Users, Customers, Brand, Supplier, Product category, Tag, Variant attribute, Register, Outlet — gives channel/location/category comparisons from one report frame.

Sales measures catalog (Advanced): COGS, Return count, Return %, Customer count, Sales-with-customer %, Items sold, Avg items per sale, Avg sale value, Avg sale value (incl. tax), First sale date, Last sale date — a near-complete basket/UPT analytics vocabulary.

Sell Through report + Dusty Inventory report + Recently Out of Stock report: the slow-mover / dead-stock / stockout trichotomy, formattable by location (move dusty stock to the store where it sells). Maps to Zevar slow-mover detection.

Inventory Replenishment report: closing inventory, items sold, days covered, reorder points, reorder amounts — and the Insights add-on adds Forecasted Demand + Suggested Reorder Quantity + Missed/Estimated items sold (lost-sale estimation for stockouts). Sales by Location report (S-Series, multi-store): bar charts of top stores by sales count and by net sales (hover = count + % of total) plus a full table (Gross Sales, Net Discounts, Net Sales, Costs, Gross Margin, Gross Profit, Taxes, per-tender Cash/Credit/Gift/Other), filterable and exportable. Reference UX for multi-store wall.

Insights marketing claims jewelry-relevant ROI: 25.4% higher GMROI and ~26% fewer out-of-stock days for top SKUs vs non-Insights users (Lightspeed's own 3-months-ended Dec-2024 methodology — vendor-sourced, treat as marketing).
profit_intelligence: Profit is exposed at the REPORT level (not a dedicated profitability cockpit): Gross profit and Margin (%) appear as selectable measures on the Retail Dashboard, Sales Summary, Individual Performance, and Sales by Location reports. COGS is an Advanced-only measure.

Lightspeed Payments report: payout, fees, and transaction amounts for accurate reconciliation — surfaces payment-processing cost, but as a reconciliation line, not absorbed into per-item margin.

No native dynamic-pricing / what-if simulation / margin-alert / AI-recommendation engine in the core product. Demand forecasting (Forecasted Demand, Suggested Reorder Quantity) exists but is strictly inventory-replenishment oriented (units to buy), NOT margin/price simulation.
Profitability dimensions supported: by Product, SKU, Category, Brand, Supplier, Outlet, Register, User — good dimensional coverage, but again as a report measure, not a guided profitability workflow.

workforce_intelligence: Individual Performance report (Pro/Advanced/Enterprise, Core/Plus): per-user table for a date range with Revenue, COGS, Gross Profit, Margin %, Tax, plus a 'Measure' dropdown to swap in Items Sold, Avg Sale Value, Customer Count, Avg Items per Sale, etc., and a 'Comparison' period. Retail Dashboard 'Top sales people' table: per-user Revenue, Sale count, Items sold, Avg sale value, Avg items per sale — a ready-made leaderboard for a workforce scoreboard.

Sales by Hour of Day is explicitly framed as a staffing-optimization report — ties workforce scheduling to demand.

cross_cutting: Tiered analytics monetization: Basic Reporting (all plans) vs Advanced Reporting (Pro/Advanced/Enterprise, Core/Plus) vs Insights add-on (Plus, or Standard/Lean/Advanced/Enterprise with add-on). Create Purchase Orders directly from inventory reports (one supplier at a time) — closes the loop from insight to action without leaving the reporting screen. Apply this 'insight -> action' pattern broadly (e.g., margin alert -> price change).

Jewelry-vertical data model primitives: serial-number tracking per piece, attachable digital certificates & appraisal records per product, variant attributes (metal type, weight, quality, ring size), barcode + RFID (via partners), layaways & deposits, dedicated Services/Repairs module with per-job assignment & status. Demand-forecasting + suggested-reorder-quantity engine in the Insights module — a real predictive-analytics feature (inventory-side), proving the platform can host ML; Zevar can extend prediction to margin/price.
Insights 'order recommendations' reflect past sales + stockouts — a closed-loop replenishment recommender.
Cross-platform access: web + iOS + Android for reporting; Pulse app for mobile live monitoring.

research_compact.category: General retail POS / omnichannel analytics platform
research_compact.region: Global (US HQ; multi-language admin); focus here on analytics/relevant to multi-store retail

live_monitoring: Live View (Analytics > Live View): real-time globe + 2D map; blue dots = visitor sessions (active in last 5 min), purple dots = orders; streamer/full-screen modes for wall displays. Metric cards: Visitors right now, Total sales (since midnight), Total sessions, Total orders, Top locations, Customers, Top products, Customer-behavior funnel over last 10 min (Active carts -> Checking out -> Purchased). Designed for BFCM-style high-traffic monitoring and immediate promo/discount impact observation. NOTE: Live View is online-store-centric; for physical POS, real-time per-store walls are weaker and typically served by third parties (Metorik, Report Pundit) — a clear gap Shopify leaves open.

Daily Sales report in POS app itself (POS Next): 3 tiles (Net sales, Average order value, Items per order) each compared to the SAME DAY LAST WEEK; date ranges Today/Yesterday/WTD/MTD/QTD/YTD; lists Top products, Top product types, Top staff at register by net sales, Net payments by type.
sales_monitoring: Retail Sales reports (only POS-channel orders; 1-5 min data latency): POS total sales by product, by product variant SKU, by vendor, by product type, Total sales by POS location, POS total sales by staff member, POS staff daily sales total, POS staff sales total (attributed to line items).
Time dimensions natively available: Day, Day of week (1-7), Hour, Hour of day (0-23), Minute, Week/Month/Quarter/Year + 'of week/year' variants — enables hourly/daily trend slicing out of the box.
Sales-over-time and Average-order-value-over-time reports (AOV formula = (gross sales - discounts) / orders).
Quantity ordered per order (basket depth / UPT proxy) + Items per order tile. Median order value discussed as a gap (AOV only).

Conversion: online conversion rate is native (sessions that completed checkout / sessions) with added-to-cart rate, reached-checkout rate, completed-checkout rate, bounce rate. IN-STORE conversion requires footfall sensors (Shopify POS does NOT count door traffic) — third-party (Hoxton AI, Dor/Aura) needed; conversion = transactions / visitors.
Sell-through rate report, Days of inventory remaining, Days in/out of stock (per location), Percent of inventory sold, Inventory units sold per day — strong slow-mover / fast-mover analytics.
Channel comparison via 'Order sales channel', 'Sales channel', 'Is POS sale' dimensions (Online Store vs POS vs Shop App, etc.) and 'POS location name' for multi-store comparison.
profit_intelligence: Gross profit and Gross margin fields IF COGS is configured (formula: gross profit = net sales - cost of goods sold; gross margin = gross profit / net sales). Net sales = gross sales - discounts - sales reversals.
Cost of goods sold per line item, average COGS, 'Net sales with cost recorded' vs 'Net sales without cost recorded' to flag products missing cost data.
Payment processing fees, average payment processing fees, FX fees, Managed Markets fees — fee-level cost visibility that jewelry tools usually lack.
Discount decomposition: line-item discounts, order-level discounts, shipping discounts, discounts from apps, discount class/method/code/name dimensions — granular markdown analytics.
Returns decomposition: gross returns, net returns, quantity returned, returned quantity rate, return fees, return reason dimension.
Inventory valuation: ending inventory value (at cost) and ending inventory retail value (at price), per location — supports carrying-cost / markdown analysis.

GAP vs jewelry: Shopify COGS is a single static number per SKU. It does NOT model gold spot-price component, gemstone cost, alloy, making/labor charge, or overhead absorption. No native margin simulation / what-if pricing. Dynamic pricing not built in.
workforce_intelligence: Staff PIN login at POS Pro locations; every transaction tied to the logged-in staff member.
'POS total sales by staff member' + 'POS staff daily sales total' + 'POS staff sales total (attributed to sale)' reports; 'Staff member name/ID' and 'Assisting staff member name/ID' dimensions.
'Rate of staff-assisted sales' and 'Percent of sales with staff help' metrics — measures personal-service impact (correlates with higher AOV).

GAP: Single-attribution only natively (one staff per sale). No multi-staff split commission, no built-in commission calculation engine, no per-associate tip breakout, no online-order staff attribution. All require third-party apps (EasyTeam, POS Staff Commission Tracker, Report Pundit). Staff sales attribution is POS Pro only (not Lite).
No native quotas/targets, no gamification, no scheduling/clock-in, no coaching/review features.
cross_cutting: Unified analytics fields model: ~400+ metrics/dimensions across B2B, Customers, Fees, Finance & payments, Fraud, Inventory, Locations, Marketing, Orders, Point of sale, Products, Sales revenue, Sessions, Stores, Time — a self-service report BUILDER with metrics + dimensions + filters + date ranges + 'Save as' custom reports.

Historical-at-time-of-sale dimensions (product title/type/vendor/variant/SKU/price 'at time of sale') so renames/reprices don't corrupt historical reports — critical for jewelry where designs/prices churn.
Product variant ABC grade dimension (A = 80% of revenue, B = 15%, C = 5%) — native ABC analysis at variant level.

Customer cohort analysis (Plus): retention rate, amount spent per customer, cumulative gross/net/total sales, orders per customer, AOV — by cohort month/week/quarter; plus PREDICTIVE spend tier and predictive amount-spent-per-customer.
RFM group dimension (recency/frequency/monetary) available for segmentation; new-or-returning-customer labeling; predicted spend tier.
ShopifyQL / Plus analytics: queryable analytics, custom dashboards, Live View + benchmarks + cohort now in the 'new analytics experience'.
Inventory fields fully location-aware (most metrics have an 'at location' twin) — engineered for multi-store from day one.
Fraud analytics (chargeback rate, fraudulent chargeback rate, fraud risk levels, Shopify Protect eligibility) — relevant to high-ticket jewelry.

Inventory model: ending_inventory_units, starting_inventory_units, days_in_stock, days_out_of_stock, sell_through_rate, days_of_inventory_remaining, ending_inventory_value (at cost), ending_inventory_retail_value (at price) — each with an 'at location' twin.

research_compact.vendor: Retail commission & sales-performance platforms (focus area: Workforce Intelligence). workforce_intelligence: COMMISSION ENGINE — flexible rule system with flat rate + tiered ranges keyed off net sale amount, discount %, OR gross-profit margin (Zevar already has Commission Rule with calculation_type Flat/By Discount Range/By Profit Margin/By Sale Amount + Commission Range child table). Vendors: Spiff, CaptivateIQ, Xactly all support tiers/accelerators/draws/SPIFs/splits/overlays in one plan. Worth adding: multi-tier ACCELERATORS tied to quota-attainment thresholds (e.g. 10% base to 100% attainment, 15% above) — CaptivateIQ documents this exact pattern; ACCELERATOR + DRAW (recoverable/non-recoverable) and SPIFF/bonus overlays; commission TRACING (Spiff) — click a payout, drill to the exact deal/line/credit that produced it; estimated-vs-realized commission projection (Spiff 'Commission Estimator').

LIVE SCOREBOARD — real-time, current-period ranking of all active associates by revenue/score. Zevar already exposes get_live_scoreboard(store_location). Industry pattern (Spinify/LevelEleven/Ambition): broadcast to an in-store TV/kiosk with auto-rotating slides, big-number cards, rank changes with motion, celebration animations on milestone hits. Add: TV/display mode in the Vue SPA (full-screen, auto-rotate store leaderboard / top deals / goal progress), configurable ranking metric (revenue, UPT, ATV, conversion, score).

PERFORMANCE SCORING — weighted multi-dimensional score. Zevar already computes overall_performance_score = revenue_weight*rev% + activity_weight*activity% + quality_weight*quality%, with guaranteed/effective/draw rate logic in run_compensation_calculation. Industry leaders (Ambition, LevelEleven) use a composite 'activity score' from controllable behaviors. Worth copying: make the scorecard CASCADING and per-role (associate vs bench jeweler vs manager each weight different KPIs); surface the score live on the associate's personal view.

QUOTA / TARGET MANAGEMENT — Performance Target doctype (period_type, revenue_target, ATV target, high-ticket target, layaway conversion target, customers-served, items-sold, repair-orders, upsell%, with revenue_weight/activity_weight). Industry: Xactly Plan and CaptivateIQ do quota allocation, territory-aware target setting, and plan-rollover automation. Worth adding: target cascading from store -> team -> associate; plan rollover/cloning; scenario/what-if target simulation before period start.

MULTI-ASSOCIATE SPLITS — Zevar supports up to 4 salespersons with split_percent on each Sales Invoice (custom_salesperson_splits -> Salesperson Split Detail). CaptivateIQ/Xactly support arbitrary splits + overlays + credit-stepping. Worth adding: split templates (default splits for common team-sale scenarios), and overlay credits for spiffs/assist without changing base split.

ATTAINMENT VISUALIZATION — progress-toward-quota gauges/rings. Spiff 'Commission Estimator' shows quota-retirement progress + projected payout; Xactly Incent shows per-deal projected earnings. Zevar has revenue_achievement_pct in get_employee_performance_summary. Worth adding: projected-on-pace (run-rate) attainment (current pace * remaining days), attainment-vs-pace line chart, and 'what do I need to hit' calculator.

COACHING WORKFLOW — Ambition and LevelEleven center on structured 1:1 coaching cadence tied to the scorecard. Zevar has Quarterly Performance Review doctype but no live coaching cadence. Worth adding: 1:1 coaching module — recurring cadence, coaching plan with SMART goals, talking-points auto-generated from scorecard gaps (e.g. 'ATV 12% below target last 3 weeks'), action-item tracking, coaching-notes history. This is the single biggest gap vs Ambition/LevelEleven.

GAMIFICATION — Spinify: points for actions (calls/demos/deals), badges, levels, contests, real-time celebrations (songs/videos/confetti). LevelEleven Motivate: contests, celebrations, company-wide alerts, live TV. Zevar has performance_log (event stream) and trending_item but no gamification layer. Worth adding: points/XP model on top of performance_log, badges for milestones (first high-ticket sale, 10 repairs closed, perfect-attendance week), time-boxed contests (e.g. 'most appraisals attached this weekend'), celebration triggers on POS events that fire to TV + associate app.

EMPLOYEE ACTIVITY FEED — Zevar has Performance Log (event_type, event_date, reference, revenue, items, customer, hours, store) and employee_live_monitor.get_my_performance (today vs yesterday). Industry pattern (Ambition 'insights', LevelEleven scorecard): per-associate daily activity scorecard with leading indicators, not just lagging revenue. Worth surfacing: today's sessions/traffic handled, appraisals created, repair intake, layaway opened, items attached, vs daily target pacing.

SCHEDULING / CLOCK-IN — Zevar performance_log records hours_worked and compensation tracks scheduled_hours vs total_hours_worked with attendance_percentage. Industry (workforce management adjacency): schedule adherence, clock-in/out, labor-vs-sales ratio. Worth tightening: clock-in events feed live 'who's on floor' for Live Monitor + schedule-adherence KPI in the scorecard.
cross_cutting: REAL-TIME DATA SYNC: Spiff/CaptivateIQ emphasize real-time commission calculation 'as deals close' and scheduled data syncs; the Zevar hook calculate_commissions on Sales Invoice on_submit already gives event-driven commission. Pattern to copy: near-real-time recalculation when returns/discounts change, with a System Activity Log / audit trail of every recalculation (Spiff 'Deep Audit Trail').

AUDIT & DISPUTE WORKFLOW: Spiff's in-app comments + notifications let rep/manager dispute a calculation in-context; Xactly emphasizes ASC 606 / IFRS 15 audit-ready expense reporting. Worth adding: comment/dispute thread on each Sales Commission Split, status lifecycle (Calculated -> Disputed -> Approved -> Paid), immutable audit log of rule edits with effective dates.

AI RECOMMENDATIONS: Spinify 'AI-driven insights to create challenges'; Ambition 'AI-powered revenue insights'. Worth adding: auto-suggested contests/spiffs based on underperforming KPIs or overstock SKUs, and AI coaching talking-points from scorecard trends.

MOBILE ACCESS: Spiff mobile app for on-the-go commission visibility; jewelry associates are floor-bound but managers roam. Worth ensuring: manager mobile view of store scoreboard + alerts; associate tablet/phone personal scoreboard.

INTEGRATION ARCHITECTURE: All leaders (Spiff, Xactly, CaptivateIQ) pull from CRM/ERP/HCM/payroll to a 'single source of truth' with ML record-matching. PER-LEADERBOARD / FLAT PRICING MODEL: Spinify charges per-leaderboard, not per-user, so cost stays flat as team grows — a packaging insight for Zevar's own pricing tiers.
CELEBRATION / RECOGNITION CHANNEL: LevelEleven company-wide alerts + Spinify personalized celebration (songs/videos) — a low-cost, high-engagement feature that lands well in a retail floor culture.

Coaching (Ambition/LevelEleven) is pipeline/activity-coaching for B2B hunters, not retail floor coaching for jewelry advisors (clienteling, relationship, product knowledge, closing high-value tickets).

None surface jewelry profitability (gold/gemstone/labor/making-charge COGS) in the commission/score equation — Zevar can tie commission to GROSS PROFIT (already supported via By Profit Margin rule), a true differentiator.

research_compact.category: Profit Intelligence & Dynamic Pricing (jewelry-specific margin/COGS modeling, price optimization, what-if simulation)

profit_intelligence: TRUE jewelry COGS breakdown (gold-at-sale + gemstone + labor/bench + making charge + alloy + commission) — model per-line AND per-invoice, capturing the gold rate AT THE POINT OF SALE (gold_rate_at_sale, gold_rate_source, gold_rate_timestamp) so historical COGS never drifts with today's spot. Zevar's sale_cost_breakdown doctype already does this; harden it.

Payment-processing cost layer per transaction: compute interchange/IC++ per tender type (credit ~2%, debit ~0.5%, Amex higher, cash ~0, financing/BNPL cost). On a $5k ring, 2% = $100, which silently erodes net margin. Zevar's _calculate_payment_costs exists; expose it in dashboards and route it into the margin waterfall.

Activity-Based Costing overhead absorption (not just 'equal per invoice' or 'proportional to revenue'): support cost pools by store (rent, utilities, insurance, marketing, depreciation, miscellaneous) + cost drivers (per-invoice, revenue-proportional, per-square-foot/display-case, per-associate-hour, per-repair-bench-hour). Zevar _allocate_overhead currently supports 2 methods; add ABC driver-based allocation as a 3rd.

Margin Waterfall (Pricefx/Vendavo pattern): List price -> metal/gold surcharge -> making charge -> discount/promo -> associate discount -> payment cost -> overhead -> NET pocket margin. Visualize the leakage at each step so owners see WHERE margin disappears.

Margin Bridge / PVM (Price-Volume-Mix) analysis (Vendavo Margin Bridge Analyzer): decompose period-over-period gross-margin change into price effect, volume effect, mix/category effect, new/discontinued effect, and cost (gold-rate) effect. This is THE killer exec view for jewelry.
Margin Heatmap by item/category/associate/store/season (Zevar get_margin_heatmap exists; extend to associate×category and store×metal).

Margin alerts & anomaly detection (Pricefx Agents pattern): continuous monitoring for margin compression (margin below floor), unusual discounting, gold-rate-spike exposure, and per-deal outliers -> actionable recommendations + approval workflows. Zevar pricing_recommendation doctype exists; wire it to live margin events.
Profit trends & cost-component trends (Zevar get_profit_trends + get_cost_component_trends exist) — extend to show gold-rate pass-through effectiveness (% of gold cost increase recovered in price).

Net-vs-gross margin separation: Gross margin (revenue - product COGS) vs Net/Contribution margin (revenue - COGS - payment cost - commission - allocated overhead). Jewelers almost never see true contribution margin per SKU today.
cross_cutting: AI recommendation engine with confidence levels + reasoning + RAG-cited similar events (Zevar Pricing Recommendation already has rag_query_id, similar_events_count, rag_sources, confidence_level, reasoning — keep and strengthen this pattern across modules).

Approval workflows for price changes / discounts / overrides (Pricefx + Vendavo pattern): configurable steps, conditions, approvers; POS override captured in pos_manager_override doctype.
Modular packaging (Pricefx Plan/Price/Profit) — sell Profit Intelligence as a tier so SMB jewelers adopt analytics before optimization.
Real-time dashboards as the default surface (customizable, role-based) — unifying all 4 Zevar modules.

Jewelry-industry margin benchmarks (gross 40-60%, net 15-25%) surfaced inline as context in dashboards (Harvest/Antoanetta benchmarks).

differentiators: Jewelry-native COGS with gold-rate-at-sale locking: most profit tools (Pricefx/Vendavo/Zilliant) are B2B-distribution generic and model COGS as a single 'cost' number; jewelry needs gold rate captured at the moment of sale because metal cost is volatile and dominates COGS. Making-charge + alloy + per-karat modeling baked in (Jewel360/Luxare/Valigara have this; generic pricing tools do not).
Gold-rate-linked dynamic pricing with live feed + fallback chain (Zevar pricing.py) — only jewelry-specific POS systems attempt this.

True contribution margin per SKU including payment interchange + commission + ABC overhead — almost no jewelry POS shows this today (Lightspeed only shows gross COGS/margin; The Edge is on-prem and basic).

AI pricing recommendations grounded in RAG over the store's own historical sales events (Zevar rag_sources) rather than black-box ML — explainability is a selling point to jewelers who distrust 'the algorithm'.

worth_copying: Pricefx Market Simulation (what-if): interactive simulator modeling real customer & competitor response to price changes via cross-price elasticity, with multi-objective optimization (revenue vs margin vs volume). Pricefx Price Waterfall: visualize and optimize every step from list price to pocket margin (gold surcharge, making, discount, payment cost, overhead). Identify exactly WHERE margin leaks.

Pricefx AI Agents pattern: continuous event-based monitoring that turns margin/discount anomalies into actionable recommendations + approval workflows, delivered via dashboards/notifications/Copilot. Replicate as Zevar margin 'Agents'.
Vendavo Profit Analyzer: in-memory analytics to slice profitability any way (by customer, product, deal, associate) and continuously surface profit opportunities.

Zilliant Margin Maximizer: AI-driven optimized prices across list, matrix, negotiated, and agreement price types that minimize over-discounting and margin leakage — adapt to jewelry's tag price / sale price / negotiated / clearance tiers.

Jewel360 metal-price tracking: (1) real-time metal price updates with suggested repricing, (2) inventory tracking by metal type/weight/purity, (3) separate markup control for metal/labor/gemstone, (4) built-in precious-metal calculator, (5) reports showing inventory value impact of metal moves. Zevar has pieces of this — productize into one UX.

Luxare 'rule-based minimum margin thresholds': define margin floors that alert OR block the sale when material cost rises above a defined floor, at POS. Critical guardrail jewelers explicitly ask for.
Valigara live gold-price-feed auto-repricing: automatically reprice catalog as spot moves, integrated with GIA/IGI certificate data — adapt for Zevar's online/catalog channel and appraisals.

Increff/RELEX markdown optimization: algorithmic clearance pricing driven by sales velocity + inventory aging + sell-through target, recommending markdown depth that maximizes recovered margin. Zevar's Clearance recommendation_type should become a real engine.

Lightspeed Margin Alert report pattern: flag items sold at cost / with zero/missing cost entered — a data-quality guardrail that prevents garbage margin reporting (very common in jewelry where cost fields are incomplete).
weaknesses: Overhead allocation is simplistic (2 methods: equal-per-invoice or revenue-proportional). Lacks Activity-Based Costing with store/department/driver granularity — fine for 1 store, weak for multi-store chains where rent and bench costs differ.

No explicit PVM / Margin Bridge analysis endpoint exists yet — get_margin_analysis groups by jewelry_type but doesn't decompose period-over-period into price/volume/mix/cost effects (the Vendavo killer view).

No what-if / market simulation endpoint — pricing_recommendation is one-shot AI; there's no interactive slider-based simulator with projected revenue/volume/margin and confidence bands (Pricefx Market Simulation gap).
No gold-rate pass-through metric or unrealized-gain/loss on inventory (historical gold cost vs replacement cost) — critical when gold breaks $4,000/oz (Luxare playbook).

No markdown/slow-mover optimization algorithm (aging buckets + sales velocity + sell-through target -> suggested clearance price). Increff/RELEX/Churchill model this; Zevar only has a 'Clearance' recommendation_type enum with no velocity math.

No multi-store profit consolidation view surfacing contribution margin per store (cost-center allocation settings exist but the profit dashboards don't clearly roll up store-vs-store with allocated overhead).

weaknesses: Most contact-center and NOC walls are operations-only (queue, SLA, MTTR) and lack revenue/profit/margin context — Zevar must fuse ops rigor WITH jewelry financials, which none do out of box.

Display/TV modes across the industry are often poorly configured (cluttered, too many widgets, unreadable at distance) — the Geckoboard teardown documents how commonly this is botched; needs opinionated defaults.
Threshold alerting is usually global, not per-store/per-context — breaks for multi-format retailers (flagship vs kiosk); Zevar must support per-store baselines.
Auto-refresh without a visible 'last updated' indicator erodes trust and hides stale/disconnected feeds (common NOC pitfall).
Public leaderboards risk demotivating or embarrassing lower performers — requires careful role/privacy design (Nextiva explicitly warns against this).
Real-time eventing across multiple stores/registers requires robust offline/resilience handling; many systems silently drop or double-count events during connectivity gaps.
